headline: Ditch Starbucks and DoorDash, George Kamel Says. How Cheap Appliances Can Replace Your Takeout Budget
summary: A daily Starbucks run and a couple of weekly DoorDash orders are two of the easiest line items to silently drain a household budget. Americans are still spending heavily on prepared food: food services spending hit $1.5 billion in March. That category, restaurants and delivery, has been climbing while grocery spending stays flat. George Kamel ... Ditch Starbucks and DoorDash, George Kamel Says. How Cheap Appliances Can Replace Your Takeout Budget

headline: Why Starbucks (SBUX) Is Up 8.2% After Mixed Q2 Results And Higher Earnings Guidance
summary: In late April 2026, Starbucks Corporation reported second-quarter revenue of US$9,531.5 million, up from US$8,761.6 million a year earlier, with net income rising to US$510.9 million from US$384.2 million, while issuing full‑year guidance that includes roughly flat consolidated net revenues and GAAP diluted EPS of US$1.73 to US$1.93. Despite higher year-on-year quarterly profit, net income for the first six months fell to US$804.2 million from US$1,165.0 million, underscoring that CEO Brian...
